target.name: AIR
target.founder: Rani Plaut, Chen Rosen, Netanel Goldberg

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
AIR is a Series A transportation company that has raised $23M to date. The valuation exercise draws from a broader transportation peer set of 8 private companies, including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger. No public comparables exist in the immediate sector. A narrow peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints within transportation—only one same-stage peer was identifiable, falling short of the three-point minimum required for reliable triangulation.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable framework here. Using the $23M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-later-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $46M. This method assumes that AIR's next financing will command a conventional markup over its current capital base, consistent with early-stage transportation venture norms.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $39M to $53M, centered on a median of $46M. This represents a relatively narrow band given reliance on a single methodological anchor. Key uncertainties include AIR's unit economics, path to revenue, and competitive differentiation relative to the fragmented transportation peer set—factors not captured in the stage-progression approach alone. A fuller valuation would benefit from revenue multiples or comparable transaction data as AIR matures toward Series B.
